description: A bound volume on a shelf and the same work read on a screen can each be a 'shelu' (book). The noun gathers an extended written work as one whole. 'penuwa' (page) is one unit within it, while 'pelua' (paper) names a material that may carry it.
articulatory_notes: Begin with the tongue blade raised behind the alveolar ridge for stressed unvoiced 'sh', then release the airflow into middle-height front 'e'. Move the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for 'l' and round the lips into high-back 'u'.

pillars.solarpunk-values: A printed book can circulate, be repaired, and remain readable without power or a network. The same noun also includes a digital book, since the work matters more than a preferred carrier.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Manuscript books carried records and stories beyond one speaker's memory. Their survival depended on durable materials and on people willing to copy, mend, and keep them.

semantic_domains.cognition: an extended written work a reader can follow beyond one sitting
semantic_domains.communication: writing gathered for readers as one work
semantic_domains.physical: a bound or otherwise embodied volume that can be held and kept
